fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer. The field is either filled automatically from a defined number series, or you enter the number manually because you have enabled manual number entry in the number-series setup.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the customer.

fields.name: Search Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate name that you can use to search for a customer.

fields.name: Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name.

fields.name: Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's address. This address will appear on all sales documents for the customer.

fields.name: Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional address information.

fields.name: City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's city.

fields.name: Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the contact person at the customer's company.

fields.name: Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's telephone number.

fields.name: Telex No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the customer's telex number. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.
Use a standard format for the telex number so that it will look uniform on printouts.

fields.name: Document Sending Profile
fields.tooltip: Specifies the preferred method of sending documents to this customer, so that you do not have to select a sending option every time that you post and send a document to the customer. Sales documents to this customer will be sent using the specified sending profile and will override the default document sending profile.

fields.name: Ship-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for another shipment address than the customer's own address, which is entered by default.

fields.name: Our Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies your account number with the customer, if you have one. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Territory Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a territory code for the customer. To see the territory codes in the Territories window, click the field.
You can use this field to allocate customers to sales personnel. For example, select the code LND in the Territory Code field for all customers from London. Later, you will be able to set a filter so the program will display only the London customers. This will make it easier for salespeople assigned to London to get an overview of the customers there.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Values window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Values window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Chain Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code indicating the name of a chain or franchise group that the customer is part of. You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Budgeted Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a budget figure for the customer.
You cannot set up budgets for customers in the same way as you can for G/L accounts. Instead, you can enter one budget figure per customer in this field. This can represent, for example, the expected profit (LCY) or sales.
This field has no relationship to any budgets in the general ledger.

fields.name: Credit Limit (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the maximum amount by which you allow the customer to exceed the payment balance before warnings are issued.
This field is used to perform a test when you enter information in journals, quotes, orders, and invoices. It tests the sales header and individual sales lines to see whether the credit limit has been exceeded.
You can post even though the credit limit has been exceeded. If the field is left blank, there will be no credit limit for this customer.
This field can also be used to print a list of customers who have exceeded their credit limit.

fields.name: Customer Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's market type to link business transactions to.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the default currency for the customer.

fields.name: Customer Price Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer price group code, which you can use to set up special sales prices in the Sales Prices window.

fields.name: Language Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the language that is used when translating specified text on documents to foreign business partner, such as an item description on an order confirmation.

fields.name: Registration Number
fields.tooltip: Specifies the registration number of the customer. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Statistics Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the statistics group.

fields.name: Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code that indicates the payment terms that you require of the customer.

fields.name: Fin. Charge Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the involved finance charges in case of late payment.

fields.name: Salesperson Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the salesperson who normally handles this customer's account.

fields.name: Shipment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which shipment method to use when you ship items to the customer.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which shipping company is used when you ship items to the customer.

fields.name: Place of Export
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the place of export that will normally be used for this customer. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.
The place of export must be declared on customs documents.

fields.name: Invoice Disc. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the invoice discount terms that you have defined for the customer.

fields.name: Customer Disc. Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer discount group code, which you can use as a criterion to set up special discounts in the Sales Line Discounts window.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: Collection Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method you normally use to collect payment from this customer (an automated bill payment service, for example). You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies an amount that is billed periodically (a subscription price, for example).

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether any comments have been entered for this customer.
Comments can concern a particular shipment method or payment problems, for example.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies which transactions with the customer that cannot be processed, for example, because the customer is insolvent.

fields.name: Invoice Copies
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many copies of a sales invoice for the customer will be printed at a time. For example, enter 2 if you always keep a copy in your files in addition to the one that you send to the customer.
You can use this field for customers that are located abroad, for example. When you export, extra copies of the printouts are always needed for customs clearance.

fields.name: Last Statement No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the last statement that was printed for this customer.

fields.name: Print Statements
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to include this customer when you print the Statement report.

fields.name: Bill-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a different customer who will be invoiced for products that you sell to the customer in the Name field on the customer card.

fields.name: Priority
fields.tooltip: Specifies a number that corresponds to the priority you give the customer. The higher the number, the higher the priority.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how the customer usually submits payment, such as bank transfer or check.

fields.name: Format Region
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Format Region to be used on printouts for this customer.

fields.name: First Transaction Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the first transaction with the customer was recorded.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the customer card was last modified.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains the starting and ending dates for a data interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the Net Change field shows the net change for that interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension so that fields such as Balance (LCY) will display the balance only for entries that relate to dimension values included in the filter.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension so that fields such as Balance (LCY) will display the balance only for entries that relate to dimension values included in the filter.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Balance
fields.tooltip:
Shows the customer's current balance. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this balance will be the sum of entries in different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the entries that make up the balance by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Balance field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or currencies.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Amount field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Balance (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment amount that the customer owes for completed sales. This value is also known as the customer's balance.

fields.name: Net Change
fields.tooltip:
Shows the net change on the customer's account for the period entered in the Date Filter field. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Net Change field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, dates and/or currencies.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Amount field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Net Change (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the net change on the customer's account for the period entered in the Date Filter field in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Amount (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.
You can filter the Net Change (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Sales (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total net amount of sales to the customer in LCY.

fields.name: Profit (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the profit in LCY.
The profit is calculated after invoicing as follows:
Unit Price - Unit Cost
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Profit (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.
You can filter the Profit (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Inv. Discounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total invoice discount granted to the customer in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Inv. Discount (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.
You can filter the Inv. Discounts (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Pmt. Discounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total payment discount granted to the customer in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Pmt. Disc. Given (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.
You can filter the Pmt. Discounts (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Balance Due
fields.tooltip:
Shows the balance due from this customer. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Balance Due field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, dates and/or currencies.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Remaining Amount field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Balance Due (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the balance due for this customer in local currency.

fields.name: Payments
fields.tooltip:
Shows the amount that this customer has paid. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Payments field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, dates and/or currencies.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Amount field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Payment.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Invoice Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the amount this customer has been invoiced for. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Invoice Amounts field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Amount field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Invoice.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Cr. Memo Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of credit memos issued to this customer. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Cr. Memo Amounts field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Amount field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Credit Memo.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Finance Charge Memo Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of finance charges that have been posted to the customer's account. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Finance Charge Memo Amounts field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Amount field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Finance Charge Memo.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Payments (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of payments received from the customer.

fields.name: Inv. Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount that has been invoiced to this customer in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Original Amt. (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Invoice.
You can filter the Inv. Amounts (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Cr. Memo Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of credit memos issued to the customer in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Original Amt. (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Credit Memo.
You can filter the Cr. Memo Amounts (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Fin. Charge Memo Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the amount of the finance charges that have been posted to the customer's account in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Original Amt. (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Finance Charge Memo.
You can filter the Fin. Charge Memo Amounts (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Outstanding Orders
fields.tooltip:
Shows the sum of the customer's outstanding orders. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Outstanding Orders field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Outstanding Amount field in the Sales Line table where the document type is Order.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Shipped Not Invoiced
fields.tooltip:
Shows the amount that has been shipped to this customer but not yet invoiced. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Amt. Shipped Not Invoiced field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Shipped Not Invoiced field in the Sales Line table where the document type is Order.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Application Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to apply payments to entries for this customer.

fields.name: Prices Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies from which location sales to this customer will be processed by default.

fields.name: Fax No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's fax number.

fields.name: Telex Answer Back
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the customer's telex answer back code. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.
Use a standard format for the telex answer back code so that it will look uniform on printouts.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's VAT registration number for customers in EU countries/regions.

fields.name: Combine Shipments
fields.tooltip: Specifies if several orders delivered to the customer can appear on the same sales invoice.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's trade type to link transactions made for this customer with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: GLN
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer in connection with electronic document sending.

fields.name: Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the post code.

fields.name: County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

fields.name: EORI Number
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Economic Operators Registration and Identification number that is used when you exchange information with the customs authorities due to trade into or out of the European Union.

fields.name: Use GLN in Electronic Document
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the GLN is used in electronic documents as a party identification number.

fields.name: Debit Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the debit entries that have been posted to the customer's account. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Debit Amount field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
To see the ledger entries that make up the debit amount, click the field.

fields.name: Credit Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the credit entries that have been posted to the customer's account. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Credit Amount field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
To see the ledger entries that make up the credit amount, click the field.

fields.name: Debit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the debit entries (in LCY) that have been posted to the customer account.
To see the ledger entries that make up the debit amount (in LCY), click the field.

fields.name: Credit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the credit entries (in LCY) that have been posted to the customer account.
To see the ledger entries that make up the credit amount (in LCY), click the field.

fields.name: E-Mail
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's email address.

fields.name: Home Page
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's home page address.

fields.name: Reminder Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how reminders about late payments are handled for this customer.

fields.name: Reminder Amounts
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of interest and additional fees that have been posted from reminders to the customer's account. If you invoice a customer in more than one currency, this amount will include different currencies. In this case, you will need to filter the amount by using a currency filter.
You can filter the Reminder Amounts field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values, currencies and/or dates.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the Amount field for those entries in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Reminder.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: Reminder Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total amount of interest and additional fees that have been posted from reminders to the customer's account in LCY.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using those entries in the Original Amt. (LCY) field in the Cust. Ledger Entry table where the document type is Reminder.
You can filter the Reminder Amounts (LCY) field so that its contents are based only on certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the customer ledger entries that make up the amount shown, click the field.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign the customer's number.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the customer or vendor is liable for sales tax.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's VAT specification to link transactions made for this customer to.

fields.name: Currency Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a currency filter so that the amounts in the Balance, Net Change, Balance Due and other FlowField fields contain entries in one or more currencies.
To see the currency codes in the Currencies window, click the field.

fields.name: Outstanding Orders (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies your expected sales income from the customer in LCY based on ongoing sales orders.

fields.name: Shipped Not Invoiced (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies your expected sales income from the customer in LCY based on ongoing sales orders where items have been shipped.

fields.name: Reserve
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether items will never, automatically (Always), or optionally be reserved for this customer. Optional means that you must manually reserve items for this customer.

fields.name: Block Payment Tolerance
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the customer is not allowed a payment tolerance.

fields.name: Pmt. Disc. Tolerance (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment amount in LCY that must be paid to get the payment discount on payments that are past the payment discount date.

fields.name: Pmt. Tolerance (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount of outstanding payment in LCY that is required to meet the payment tolerance conditions.

Additional Information

You can use payment tolerance so that every outstanding amount has a set maximum allowed payment tolerance. If the payment tolerance is met, then the payment amount is analyzed as follows:

If the payment amount is an underpayment, then the outstanding amount is fully closed by the underpayment. A detailed ledger entry is posted to the payment entry so that no remaining amount is left on the applied invoice entry.


If the payment amount is an overpayment, then a new detailed ledger entry is posted to the payment entry so that no remaining amount is left on the payment entry.

fields.name: IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's intercompany partner code.

fields.name: Refunds
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of transactions with document type Refund.

fields.name: Refunds (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of refunds received from the customer.

fields.name: Other Amounts
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of transactions where no document type is defined.

fields.name: Other Amounts (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum in LCY of transactions where no document type is defined.

fields.name: Prepayment %
fields.tooltip: Specifies a prepayment percentage that applies to all orders for this customer, regardless of the items or services on the order lines.

fields.name: Outstanding Invoices (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies your expected sales income from the customer in LCY based on unpaid sales invoices.

fields.name: Outstanding Invoices
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the sum of the customer's outstanding sales invoices.
The sum is calculated and updated based on values in the Outstanding Amount field on sales lines for the customer.

fields.name: Bill-to No. Of Archived Doc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of archived sales orders that have been registered for the customer when the customer acts as the bill-to customer.

fields.name: Sell-to No. Of Archived Doc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of archived sales orders that have been registered for the customer when the customer acts as the sell-to customer.

fields.name: Partner Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies for direct debit collections if the customer that the payment is collected from is a person or a company.

fields.name: Intrastat Partner Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies for Intrastat reporting if the customer is a person or a company.

fields.name: Exclude from Pmt. Practices
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the customer must be excluded from Payment Practices calculations.

fields.name: Image
fields.tooltip: Specifies the picture of the customer, for example, a logo.

fields.name: Privacy Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to limit access to data for the data subject during daily operations. This is useful, for example, when protecting data from changes while it is under privacy review.

fields.name: Disable Search by Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies that you can change the customer name on open sales documents. The change applies only to the documents.

fields.name: Allow Multiple Posting Groups
fields.tooltip: Specifies if multiple posting groups can be used for posting business transactions for this customer.

fields.name: Preferred Bank Account Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's bank account that will be used by default when you process refunds to the customer and direct debit collections.

fields.name: Coupled to Dataverse
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the customer is coupled to an account in Dataverse.

fields.name: Cash Flow Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a payment term that will be used to calculate cash flow for the customer.

fields.name: Primary Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the contact number for the customer.

fields.name: Mobile Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the customer's mobile telephone number.

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the responsibility center that will administer this customer by default.

fields.name: Shipping Advice
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the customer accepts partial shipment of orders.

fields.name: Shipping Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies how long it takes from when the items are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered.

fields.name: Shipping Agent Service Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the shipping agent service to use for this customer.

fields.name: Price Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the default price calculation method.

fields.name: Allow Line Disc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies if a sales line discount is calculated when a special sales price is offered according to setup in the Sales Prices window.

fields.name: No. of Quotes
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of sales quotes that have been registered for the customer.

fields.name: No. of Blanket Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of sales blanket orders that have been registered for the customer.

fields.name: No. of Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of sales orders that have been registered for the customer.

fields.name: No. of Invoices
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of unposted sales invoices that have been registered for the customer.

fields.name: No. of Return Orders
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of sales return orders that have been registered for the customer.

fields.name: No. of Credit Memos
